title: Pandemic Inflation: What $1,000 in Jan 2020 Is Worth Today
description: The purchasing power of $1,000 at the start of 2020 relative to 2025, using US CPI-U data. This covers the COVID-era inflation surge (2021-2022) which was the highest sustained inflation in the US since the early 1980s.
data_source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics, CPI-U seasonally unadjusted
educational_note: The COVID-era inflation eroded ~18% of cash purchasing power in just 5 years. This is why the Federal Reserve raised interest rates from 0.25% to 5.50% between March 2022 and July 2023 — the fastest rate-hiking cycle in 40 years. Investors in broad equity funds largely kept pace with or exceeded inflation during this period.
